description_long: This object is a vintage wooden television set with a boxy, rectangular form and a rich, reddish-brown wood grain finish. The front features a large, convex glass screen framed by ornate wooden columns and a decorative floral motif along the base. To the right of the screen, there is a control panel with a grid-patterned speaker, several colored buttons, and a numeric keypad, all set into a metallic-looking section. The sides and back are plain, showcasing the wood texture and the same decorative floral band near the base. The top is flat and broad, suitable for placing small items. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century console televisions, blending functional electronics with decorative furniture aesthetics.
description: A vintage wooden console television with a large glass screen, ornate floral base, and a side control panel featuring buttons and a speaker.
description_view_0: The back view shows a plain wooden surface with a decorative floral band near the base, lacking any visible controls or screen.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the wood grain and floral base, with no visible screen or controls, emphasizing the boxy shape.
description_view_2: The front view reveals the television's glass screen, ornate wooden columns, floral base, and a control panel with buttons and a speaker on the right.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wood grain and floral base, with no visible screen or controls.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, wooden, reddish-brown with ornate floral carvings at the base
attribute_control_button_layout: right side of the front panel, arranged in a cluster with a numeric keypad and colored buttons
